tr.td: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Human isophane insulin 70 unit/mL and recombinant human regular insulin 30 unit/mL solution for injection
Inject 10 units (70/30 strength) subcutaneously once daily
Ibuprofen is not recommended due to chronic kidney disease and comorbidities.
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Metformin hydrochloride 500 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Alendronic acid (as alendronate sodium) 10 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
400 micrograms (1 spray) under the tongue as needed for chest pain, may repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Vitamin B12 and/or vitamin B12 derivative-containing product
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Depression screening using Patient Health Questionnaire Two-Item score (procedure)
Assessment using AUDIT-C (Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test - Consumption)
Inadequate digital access affecting health care

ul.li: Achieve and maintain LDL cholesterol below 70 mg/dL to reduce cardiovascular risk.
Achieve and maintain fasting triglycerides below 150 mg/dL.
Patient will achieve and maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will demonstrate understanding of recommended lifestyle changes for hypertension, including diet, exercise, and sodium reduction.
Patient will implement and maintain a diabetes self-management plan including dietary modifications, regular physical activity, blood glucose monitoring, and medication adherence as appropriate.
Patient will achieve and maintain hemoglobin A1c within individualized target range to reduce risk of diabetes complications.

tr.td: Vaccine product containing only Influenza virus antigen (medicinal product)

Patient will achieve and maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Patient will demonstrate understanding of recommended lifestyle changes for hypertension, including diet, exercise, and sodium reduction.

Patient will achieve and maintain hemoglobin A1c within individualized target range to reduce risk of diabetes complications.
